grounding_source_rows: taxonomy: Criminal Law > DUII/DWS vs DMV (DUII Related) vs Administrative Law > DMV (Non-Criminal); v1 dui_vs_dmv pair
grounding_note: 'I lost my license' spans three exact labels the taxonomy carefully separates: the criminal DUII charge itself, the parallel administrative implied-consent suspension (DMV DUII Related — with its own short hearing deadline), and non-criminal bureaucratic license actions (Administrative Law > DMV). Which notice the user received is a paper fact they hold. v1 tested this pair but with facts that restated the whole scenario; here the opening stays on the license problem and the disclosure identifies the process.

scenarios.opening_query: I'm about to lose my driver's license and I need it for work. Please tell me what my options are.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Administrative Law > DMV (Non-Criminal) || Criminal Law > DMV (DUII Related) || Criminal Law > DUII/DWS
scenarios.hidden_fact: I got a 'notice of intent to suspend' after a DUII arrest — I have ten days to request the implied-consent hearing.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's the notice of intent to suspend from my DUII arrest — I've got ten days to request the hearing.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: what kind of notice triggered the license action
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The DMV refused to renew because of my age and a vision report — no arrest, no criminal anything.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: They refused my renewal over my age and a vision report — nothing criminal.
scenarios.vetting_note: The implied-consent hearing deadline is the most time-critical routing fact in the whole taxonomy.

scenarios.opening_query: I was pulled over Saturday night and now everything is a mess. I've never been in trouble before.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Criminal Law > Traffic Offenses || Criminal Law > DUII/DWS
scenarios.hidden_fact: They arrested me for DUII — I blew just over the limit after two glasses of wine.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: I was arrested for DUII — blew just over the limit after two glasses of wine.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the stop produced a criminal DUII arrest or a citation
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It was a speeding ticket — 20 over — plus a lane-change violation. No alcohol involved.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: Just speeding — 20 over — and a lane-change violation. No alcohol.
scenarios.vetting_note: 'Pulled over, everything's a mess' is authentically vague; arrest-vs-citation is the severity fact.

scenarios.opening_query: I need help with my DUI case. This whole thing has been a nightmare for my family.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Criminal Law > DUII/DWS || Criminal Law > DMV (DUII Related)
scenarios.hidden_fact: The criminal side is done — I completed diversion. What's left is the DMV hearing over my license suspension.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The criminal case is done — I completed diversion. What's left is the DMV suspension hearing.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the criminal case or the administrative suspension is the live issue
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The criminal charge is very much alive — arraignment is next week and I need a defense lawyer.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: The charge is live — arraignment's next week and I need a defense lawyer.
scenarios.vetting_note: Users call the whole cluster 'my DUI case'; the resolved-vs-pending fact isolates the administrative remnant.

scenarios.opening_query: My license got suspended and I honestly don't understand why or how to fix it.
scenarios.hidden_fact: It turned out to be an unpaid court judgment from an old car accident — the suspension lifts only when the judgment is handled.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's from an unpaid judgment from an old car accident — the suspension only lifts when that's dealt with.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the suspension stems from an unpaid judgment rather than a DMV decision
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It was a DMV paperwork error — they merged my record with someone else's who has the same name.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's a DMV error — they merged my record with another person with my name.
scenarios.vetting_note: Judgment-based suspensions are a debtor problem wearing a DMV costume; row 212's uninsured-accident aftermath.

scenarios.opening_query: I got a ticket last week and I'm worried about what it means for my job.
scenarios.final: Criminal Law > Traffic Offenses - CDL Drivers
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Criminal Law > Traffic Offenses || Criminal Law > Traffic Offenses - CDL Drivers
scenarios.hidden_fact: I drive a semi for a living — I hold a CDL, and even this ticket in my personal car counts against it.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: I'm a truck driver with a CDL — even a ticket in my personal car counts against it.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the driver holds a commercial license
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: I just commute in my sedan — no commercial license; I'm worried about insurance rates.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: I just commute — no CDL. I'm mostly worried about insurance rates.
scenarios.vetting_note: CDL status is the taxonomy's stated reason for the separate label; 'worried about my job' invites the question.

scenarios.opening_query: I was cited for driving while suspended but I truly didn't know my license was suspended.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Criminal Law > DUII/DWS || Administrative Law > DMV (Non-Criminal)
scenarios.hidden_fact: The citation was dismissed once I showed proof — the remaining fight is with the DMV, whose record error caused the phantom suspension.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The citation got dismissed with my proof — what's left is fixing the DMV record error that caused the phantom suspension.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the criminal citation is resolved leaving only the agency record dispute
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The DWS charge is still active with a court date next month — I need defense on it.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: The DWS charge is active — court date next month.
scenarios.vetting_note: Resolved-charge fact leaves a pure records-correction problem; posture, not conduct, is hidden.

scenarios.opening_query: My teenage son got his first ticket and my insurance company is already threatening to drop us.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Criminal Law > Traffic Offenses || Criminal Law > Juvenile Offender
scenarios.hidden_fact: It's more than a ticket — he's 16 and charged in juvenile court with reckless driving and attempting to elude after a street-racing stop.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's worse than a ticket — he's 16, in juvenile court for reckless driving and attempting to elude from a street-racing stop.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the minor faces juvenile-court charges beyond a citation
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's a simple speeding ticket — 15 over on the highway; we just want to contest it.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: Just a speeding ticket, 15 over — we want to contest it.
scenarios.vetting_note: Parent minimization ('first ticket') hiding juvenile charges is a documented intake pattern.

scenarios.opening_query: I refused the breathalyzer when I got stopped and now I'm dealing with the fallout.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Criminal Law > DUII/DWS || Criminal Law > DMV (DUII Related)
scenarios.hidden_fact: The DA declined to file charges — the only thing left is the year-long refusal suspension, and my hearing window closes Friday.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The DA didn't file charges — what's left is the refusal suspension, and my hearing window closes Friday.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether criminal charges were filed or only the administrative refusal suspension remains
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: I was charged with DUII on top of the refusal — both cases are running at once.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: They charged the DUII too — both cases are going at once.
scenarios.vetting_note: Refusal cases split precisely here; declination is a fact users learn and can report.

scenarios.opening_query: The DMV took my disabled parking permit away and I can barely walk a block.
scenarios.final: General Litigation > Americans With Disabilities Act
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Administrative Law > DMV (Non-Criminal) || General Litigation > Americans With Disabilities Act
scenarios.hidden_fact: It's part of something bigger — the county revoked accessible parking around my building too, and I want a disability-rights case, not just the permit back.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's bigger than the permit — the county removed the accessible parking near my building too. I want a disability-rights case.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the user seeks the agency decision reversed or broader disability-access relief
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's just the permit denial — my new doctor's form was rejected on a technicality and I want it appealed.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: Just the permit — they rejected my doctor's form on a technicality and I want that appealed.
scenarios.vetting_note: Permit denial is the taxonomy's own DMV example; systemic-access facts escalate to ADA.

scenarios.opening_query: I have to deal with a DMV hearing about my driving and I don't want to go in alone.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Criminal Law > DMV (DUII Related) || Administrative Law > DMV (Non-Criminal)
scenarios.hidden_fact: It's a medical-review hearing — my neurologist reported my seizure and they're deciding whether I keep driving. No alcohol, no arrest, ever.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's a medical review — my neurologist reported my seizure and they're deciding if I keep my license. Nothing alcohol-related, ever.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: what kind of DMV hearing it is
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's the implied-consent hearing from my DUII stop last month.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's the implied-consent hearing from my DUII stop.
scenarios.vetting_note: Medical-review hearings are pure administrative process; 'DMV hearing' alone triggers the DUII default.
